channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ladakh Civil Society Groups Reach Preliminary Agreement with Centre on Constitutional Safeguards and Democracy Restoration
item.description: In a significant development for the Union Territory of Ladakh, two major civil society organisations namely the Leh Apex Body (LAB) and Kargil Democratic Alliance (KDA) have announced that they reach...

item.title: Stella Clarifies to Vincent Why She Spoke with Matthew in Episode 48 of The Alibi
item.description: In Episode 48 of the popular Philippine TV series 'The Alibi,' Stella addresses Vincent to clarify the reasons behind her recent conversation with Matthew. The discussion aims to clear any misundersta...

item.title: NCCIA arrests four over illegal sale of government officials’ personal data
item.description: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) on Friday announced the arrest of four individuals linked to an organised network in South Punjab involved in trading sensitive personal data of g...

item.title: International Community Condemns Drone Strikes Targeting Civilians in DR Congo
item.description: The International Contact Group for the Great Lakes region has strongly condemned drone attacks on civilian populations in the Democratic Republic of Congo, calling it a dangerous escalation in the on...

item.title: Jollibee Faces Financial Challenges Amid MSCI Index Downgrade
item.description: Jollibee Foods Corporation was recently downgraded from the MSCI Philippines Standard Index to the small-cap category, signaling caution for global investors rather than a financial collapse. While th...

item.title: Stephen Hawking's former Cambridge home listed for sale at £2.5 million
item.description: The five-bedroom residence in the Newnham area of Cambridge, once home to the renowned physicist Stephen Hawking, has been put on the market for £2.5 million. Hawking, who passed away in 2018 aged 76,...

item.title: U.S. directs all travelers from Ebola-affected regions to enter through Washington-Dulles Airport
item.description: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security has implemented a new measure requiring all travelers returning from countries affected by Ebola to enter the United States through a single designated airport...

item.title: Introducing XONARA: Insights Into the P-pop Girl Group and Their Debut Track “TABI”
item.description: XONARA, a rising P-pop girl group, recently made their debut with their first single titled “TABI.” The group aims to establish a distinct identity within the Philippine pop music scene. Their name, X...

item.title: Senator Ahmed Wadada emerges APC governorship candidate in Nasarawa State
item.description: Senator Ahmed Aliyu Wadada, wey dey represent Nasarawa West Senatorial District, don win the All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship primary election for Nasarawa State. The announcement come on ...

item.title: Police Investigate Alleged Election Irregularities Involving Tameside Labour Candidate
item.description: A former Labour campaigner has claimed that a plan to influence the outcome of local elections by introducing fake candidates was discussed during a social gathering in Angela Rayner's home, though sh...

item.title: SpaceX Successfully Launches Starship 12 in Key Test for Future Lunar Missions
item.description: SpaceX has successfully launched Starship Flight 12, marking a major milestone in the development of the largest and most powerful rocket ever built. The launch took place at the company's Starbase fa...

item.title: Senate candidate dismisses staffer over offensive post targeting rival's spouse
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins, a Republican Senate candidate from Georgia, dismissed Brandon Phillips, a senior staffer on his campaign, after a controversial post from the campaign's 'War Room' account targeted ...

item.title: Africa Reaffirms Support for One-China Principle Amid Taiwan-Separatist Moves
item.description: Recent political moves by Lai Ching-te, aimed at seeking international support for Taiwan's independence, have encountered significant resistance, particularly from Africa. eSwatini, the only African ...

item.title: Experts Raise Security Concerns Over Mandatory White House App on Government Devices
item.description: The Trump administration has directed federal agencies to install a new White House app on millions of government-issued devices, including iPhones and iPads. Launched in March, the app offers direct ...

item.title: Maharashtra Leads India’s Infrastructure Growth with Integrated Development Approach
item.description: During The Hindu’s Maharashtra Infrastructure Conclave 2026 held in Mumbai, M. Jamshed emphasised that infrastructure is not just a component but essentially synonymous with development itself. He hig...

item.title: Maku and Two Presidential Aspirants Cleared for Labour Party Primaries
item.description: On Friday, former Minister of Information, Labaran Maku, alongside two Labour Party presidential aspirants, Peter Agada and Samuel Nwigwe, successfully passed the party's screening exercise ahead of t...

item.title: US Intelligence Official Tulsi Gabbard Resigns Amid White House Pressure
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, a senior intelligence official in the United States, has resigned from her position following pressure from the White House, according to a source familiar with the situation. Gabbard p...

item.title: Top Memorial Day Sales and Discounts for 2026
item.description: CNN Underscored has curated a comprehensive guide to the best Memorial Day 2026 deals across a wide range of products and retailers. The guide highlights 172 top offers, including discounts on tech ga...

item.title: Dr Yaseen Bismilla uses TikTok to educate on forensic pathology and justice
item.description: South African forensic pathologist Dr Yaseen Bismilla is transforming public understanding of medicine, death, and forensic science through TikTok. With a growing online following, he blends education...

item.title: Mercedes secures front row for Canadian Grand Prix sprint with Russell and Antonelli
